title: [lichen_l2.c:249-252] atomic_t memory ordering comment warns about multi-core but code may run on multi-core
description:
Category not checked: Memory ordering on multi-core systems

Bug found: The comment at lines 249-252 says:
'Note: Zephyr's atomic_set()/atomic_get() do NOT provide memory ordering
guarantees. On single-core Cortex-M this is acceptable since stores are
naturally ordered. Multi-core would require explicit barriers.'

However, Zephyr supports multi-core systems (nRF53 with dual cores, ESP32-S3 with dual cores). The comment acknowledges the issue but the code provides no solution.

If this code runs on a multi-core system:
1. Core 0 calls atomic_set(&link_ctx_initialized, 1) after filling link_ctx
2. Core 1 reads atomic_get(&link_ctx_initialized) == 1
3. Core 1 accesses link_ctx fields
4. WITHOUT a memory barrier, Core 1 may see stale values in link_ctx due to cache coherency delays

The Zephyr docs for atomic operations say they are 'not memory barriers by themselves'. For multi-core safety, __sync_synchronize() or similar would be needed.

Impact: P3 - Potential data race on multi-core Zephyr targets.
close_reason: Fixed: Added compile-time BUILD_ASSERT that rejects CONFIG_SMP or CONFIG_MP_MAX_NUM_CPUS > 1 builds. The comment now clearly documents the single-core platform constraint and why memory barriers are not used.
